sourceMovie.title: Oppenheimer
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Oppenheimer (2023) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Christopher Nolan's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and history blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Richard Jewell
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Oppenheimer clicked for you, Richard Jewell (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Oppenheimer?
faq.answer: Try Richard Jewell, The Zone of Interest, Radioactive—each shares DNA with Oppenheimer while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Oppenheimer?
faq.answer: Oppenheimer (2023) is best described as Drama, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Oppenheimer worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
